title: BA Persona Trinity - Consultant Architect Diplomat
content: BA combines three personas: Consultant with client focus value driver advisor role and trust builder; Architect with systems view structure abstraction and decomposition; Diplomat with negotiation facilitation conflict management and consensus building. Together they form the Requirements Engineering Expert.

title: Enterprise BA Persona
content: Enterprise BA focuses on strategic alignment and organizational transformation. Works at portfolio level connecting business strategy to solution investments. Key concerns: enterprise architecture alignment; cross-departmental impact; long-term business capability building; governance and standards compliance.

title: Project BA Persona
content: Project BA focuses on delivering specific project requirements within scope time and budget. Works at project level with PM and delivery team. Key concerns: requirements elicitation and documentation; stakeholder management within project; change control; traceability; UAT support.

title: IT BA Persona
content: IT BA bridges business needs and technical implementation. Works closely with development and architecture teams. Key concerns: technical feasibility; system integration requirements; data models; API specifications; non-functional requirements; technical documentation quality.

title: Agile BA Persona
content: Agile BA works in sprint-based environments as product owner partner or team BA. Key concerns: user story writing; backlog refinement; acceptance criteria; sprint planning; continuous elicitation; just-enough documentation; value delivery per sprint; WSJF prioritization.

title: UX BA Persona
content: UX BA combines business analysis with user experience design. Key concerns: user research; persona development; journey mapping; usability requirements; prototyping; design thinking facilitation; translating user needs into functional requirements with empathy.

title: CBAP Certification - BABOK v3
content: CBAP requires BABOK v3 knowledge equivalent to 7500 plus hours BA experience. Six BABOK knowledge areas: Business Analysis Planning and Monitoring; Elicitation and Collaboration; Requirements Life Cycle Management; Strategy Analysis; Requirements Analysis and Design Definition; Solution Evaluation.

title: IREB CPRE Certification
content: IREB CPRE covers Foundation plus Advanced levels in Elicitation and Management. Standards basis: ISO IEC IEEE 29148 for requirements engineering life cycle processes and information items. Focus areas: requirements elicitation techniques; specification quality; requirements management; change management.

title: PMI-PBA Certification
content: PMI-PBA covers Predictive and Agile BA competencies. Focus areas: Needs Assessment; Planning; Analysis; Traceability and Monitoring; Evaluation. Bridges traditional waterfall and agile approaches. Recognized by project management community alongside PMP credential.

title: Consulting Mindset Principle 1 - Client-Centric Thinking
content: Client success equals BA success. Behaviors: understand client business context before diving into details; speak client language not technical jargon; always connect requirements to business value; measure success by client outcomes not deliverables produced. The deliverable is not the goal - the outcome is.

title: Consulting Mindset Principle 2 - Trusted Advisor Status
content: Trust formula: Credibility plus Reliability plus Intimacy divided by Self-Orientation. Credibility: demonstrate expertise through insights not claims. Reliability: deliver on promises every time. Intimacy: create psychological safety for honest conversations. Low Self-Orientation: focus on their needs not your agenda.

title: Consulting Mindset Principle 3 - Hypothesis-Driven Approach
content: Do not just gather information but test hypotheses. Steps: form hypotheses about the problem before interviews; design questions to validate or invalidate hypotheses; update understanding based on evidence; be willing to be wrong as that is learning. Hypothesis-driven elicitation is more efficient.

title: Consulting Mindset Principle 4 - Value-First Mindset
content: Every requirement must answer: what value does this create? Behaviors: challenge requirements without clear value; quantify benefits where possible; prioritize ruthlessly based on value divided by effort; kill scope creep by returning to value conversation. Value-first prevents feature bloat.

title: Power Interest Matrix for Stakeholders
content: Four quadrants: High Power High Interest - Manage Closely with regular 1-on-1s and key decisions; High Power Low Interest - Keep Satisfied with no surprises; Low Power High Interest - Keep Informed with regular communications and workshops; Low Power Low Interest - Monitor with minimal effort.

title: RACI Matrix for BA Activities
content: RACI assignment for BA activities: Define Business Need - Sponsor Accountable BA Responsible; Elicit Requirements - User SME Accountable BA Responsible; Write Requirements - BA Responsible and Accountable; Review Requirements - Tech Lead Accountable BA Responsible; Approve Requirements - Sponsor Accountable PM Responsible.

title: Stakeholder Categories Framework
content: Seven stakeholder categories: Sponsors concerned with ROI and strategic alignment; Domain SMEs concerned with business rules and workflows; End Users concerned with usability and daily tasks; Technical concerned with feasibility and integration; Regulatory concerned with compliance; Support concerned with maintainability; External vendors and partners.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 1 - Planning and Monitoring
content: Tasks: Plan BA Approach choosing predictive vs adaptive vs hybrid; Plan Stakeholder Engagement; Plan BA Governance; Plan BA Information Management; Identify BA Performance Improvements. Key deliverables: BA Plan; Stakeholder Engagement Approach; Governance Approach document.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 2 - Elicitation and Collaboration
content: Tasks: Prepare for Elicitation with research questions and logistics; Conduct Elicitation via interview workshop observation and analysis; Confirm Elicitation Results by validating understanding; Communicate BA Information to all stakeholders; Manage Stakeholder Collaboration throughout project.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 3 - Requirements Life Cycle Management
content: Tasks: Trace Requirements backward and forward; Maintain Requirements with version baseline and status; Prioritize Requirements by value effort and risk; Assess Requirements Changes with impact analysis; Approve Requirements through formal sign-off process. Foundation for change control.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 4 - Strategy Analysis
content: Tasks: Analyze Current State with as-is processes and pain points; Define Future State with to-be vision and success metrics; Assess Risks by identifying analyzing and planning responses; Define Change Strategy with roadmap and transition approach. Connects business strategy to requirements.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 5 - Requirements Analysis and Design Definition
content: Tasks: Specify and Model Requirements covering FR NFR data and process; Verify Requirements with quality checks and reviews; Validate Requirements with stakeholder confirmation; Define Requirements Architecture; Define Design Options as solution alternatives; Analyze Potential Value with cost-benefit and ROI; Allocate Requirements to components and releases.

title: BABOK Knowledge Area 6 - Solution Evaluation
content: Tasks: Measure Solution Performance with KPIs comparing actuals to targets; Analyze Performance Measures with variance analysis; Assess Solution Limitations identifying constraints and gaps; Assess Enterprise Limitations for organizational readiness; Recommend Actions for improvements or replacements. Closes the BA value loop.

title: Core Competencies Self-Assessment Framework
content: Competency areas with 1-5 scale: Analytical Thinking subtotal 30 points; Communication Skills subtotal 35 points; Behavioral Characteristics subtotal 30 points; Business Knowledge subtotal 25 points; Technical Skills subtotal 25 points. Total 145 points. 80 percent plus is Expert BA; 60-79 percent is Competent BA.

title: BA Core Responsibilities Six Areas
content: Six responsibility areas: Elicitation and Discovery with interviews workshops and observation; Analysis and Modeling with decomposition and gap identification; Specification and Documentation with BRD SRS FRD; Validation and Quality Assurance with reviews and UAT support; Stakeholder Management with conflict resolution; Value Realization with post-implementation review.

title: Interaction Principles - Always and Never
content: Always: be neutral and objective; listen actively and paraphrase; verify understanding before assuming; respect all stakeholder perspectives; document everything; focus on needs not solutions; ask why and so what; use examples and scenarios; confirm next steps; follow up promptly. Never: take sides; make unverified assumptions; promise what cannot be delivered.

title: Adaptive Communication Matrix
content: Communication styles by stakeholder: C-Level Executive - brief high-level outcome-focused monthly with exec summary; Project Sponsor - strategic decision-oriented weekly status report; Business SME - collaborative example-driven as needed working sessions; IT Development - technical precise daily specs; End User - simple visual empathetic demos; Compliance - formal documented per requirement.

title: Stakeholder Empathy Map Template
content: Empathy map sections for each key stakeholder: Thinks and Feels - what really matters to them; Hears - what colleagues and management tell them; Sees - what they observe in their environment; Says - what they tell you and others; Does - actual behavior and workarounds; Pains - fears frustrations and obstacles; Gains - what they want to achieve.

title: Consultant Toolkit - Problem Analysis Tools
content: Tools: Issue Tree to decompose complex problems at project start when problem is ambiguous; MECE Principle ensuring complete non-overlapping analysis when categorizing requirements; 5 Whys to find root cause when symptom is clear; So What Test ensuring insights are actionable after every analysis; Pyramid Principle for structuring executive communication.

title: BA Skill-01 Pre-Work Checklist
content: Before any BA work verify: Persona Setup - consulting mindset activated; role clarity established; value-first thinking enabled. Stakeholder Analysis - all categories identified; power interest mapping completed; empathy maps created; RACI defined; communication strategy set. Competency Check - self-assessment done; domain knowledge refreshed; appropriate skills loaded.

title: BA Authority vs Credibility Matrix
content: BA has no formal authority over stakeholders. Credibility sources: Expertise power from deep preparation and domain knowledge; Relationship power from genuine rapport and reliability; Information power from being knowledge hub; Process power from controlling agenda and framework; Coalition power from pre-aligned alliances with decision makers.

title: ISO Standards for BA Practice
content: Key ISO standards: ISO IEC IEEE 29148:2018 for requirements engineering life cycle; ISO IEC 25010:2011 SQuaRE model for NFR quality characteristics; ISO IEC 25012:2008 for 15 data quality characteristics; ISO 9241-11:2018 for usability effectiveness efficiency and satisfaction; CMMI DEV v2.0 for process maturity.

title: Professional Identity Statement Template
content: BA professional identity combines: role as Requirements Engineering Expert with 15 plus years equivalent experience; certification standards IREB CBAP PMI-PBA ISO 29148; methodology coverage from waterfall to agile SAFe 6.0 and BPMN 2.0; value proposition connecting business needs to technology solutions through structured analysis.
